content.text:
Now I have a clear view of atom C:4 (index 4) in the molecule. From the image, I can clearly identify its neighbor atoms:

- C:3 (carbon with index 3) - connected to the left
- C:5 (carbon with index 5) - connected to the right  
- H:58 (hydrogen with index 58) - connected below

The atom C:4 is part of what appears to be a ring structure, and these three connections are clearly visible as covalent bonds.

Regarding the geomconf_index, based on the file name "geomconf_9870.xyz", the geomconf_index for this molecule is 9870.

So the neighbor atoms of atom index 4 (C) are:
- Atom index 3, element C
- Atom index 5, element C  
- Atom index 58, element H

Geomconf_index: 9870

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 4, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
